Aktives Portfolio.description: Contracts or accounts whose lifecycle status represents an active customer relationship.
Aktives Portfolio.date_basis: Use the fact table's current contract_status_sk unless the question explicitly asks for historical status.
Aktives Portfolio.notes: Join the relevant fact table to dim_contract_status via contract_status_sk.

Ausfallquote.description: Share of defaulted contracts among all considered contracts.
Ausfallquote.formula: COUNT(CASE WHEN dim_contract_status.is_defaulted_flag THEN 1 END) / COUNT(contract_id)
Ausfallquote.date_basis: Use the reporting period requested by the question.
Ausfallquote.notes: Join to dim_contract_status and filter the relevant portfolio or reporting period explicitly.

Available Balance.description: Account balance available after holds, disputes, compliance blocks, and limits.
Available Balance.notes: Use this instead of current_balance_net when the question asks what is effectively available.

Approved Overdraft Facility.description: An account snapshot with an approved authorized overdraft amount.
Approved Overdraft Facility.date_basis: Use snapshot_date_sk only when the question constrains account snapshots by time.
Approved Overdraft Facility.notes: Use authorized_overdraft_limit_net for the approved facility. Use available_balance_effective < 0 only for actual overdrawn state.

Blocked Funds.description: Funds blocked on an account snapshot due to holds, disputes, or compliance restrictions.
Blocked Funds.date_basis: Use snapshot_date_sk only when the question constrains account snapshots by time.
Blocked Funds.notes: Use blocked_amount_effective for blocked funds. Do not substitute available_balance_effective unless the question asks for available balance.

Business Customer.description: A corporate or business customer in the customer dimension.
Business Customer.date_basis: Use dim_customer.is_current_record = TRUE only when the question asks for current customers.
Business Customer.notes: In this synthetic warehouse, business customer wording maps to customer_type_code = 'Corporate'. Do not use the literal value 'Business' because it is not a generated customer_type_code.

BEV Share.description: Share of vehicles with a battery-electric fuel type in the selected population.
BEV Share.formula: COUNT(CASE WHEN dim_vehicle.fuel_type_code = 'BEV' THEN 1 END) / COUNT(*)
BEV Share.date_basis: Use the related contract origination or start date when measuring financed vehicles in a period.
BEV Share.notes: BEV is represented by dim_vehicle.fuel_type_code = 'BEV'. If the question says active contract portfolio, count distinct vehicles linked to active leasing or loan contracts via dim_contract_status.is_active_flag = TRUE.

Cash Realization Rate.description: Ratio of realized non-reversed gross cash receipts to planned gross cash due.
Cash Realization Rate.date_basis: Use due_date_sk for planned cashflows and value_date_sk for actual receipts unless the question constrains matured contracts.
exclusions: Reversed payment transactions must not count as realized cash receipts.
Cash Realization Rate.notes: In this synthetic warehouse, reversal rows are booked with the opposite sign of the original and point to it via reverses_payment_transaction_sk.

Cash Realization After Reversals.description: Cash realization where successful original inflows and signed reversal rows are both included to measure net realized cash after reversals.
Cash Realization After Reversals.formula: SUM(successful original inflows plus signed reversal rows) / NULLIF(SUM(planned gross cashflows), 0)
Cash Realization After Reversals.date_basis: Use due_date_sk for planned cashflows. Use value_date_sk of the original payment for receipt timing unless the question explicitly asks for reversal booking timing.
Cash Realization After Reversals.notes: Use is_reversal_flag = FALSE for original successful inflows. Include is_reversal_flag = TRUE rows only when the question explicitly asks for signed reversals or reversal-adjusted accounting; reversal rows carry the opposite sign in amount_gross and reference the original via reverses_payment_transaction_sk.

Cross-Product Cannibalization.description: Customers replacing an early-terminated lease with a new loan for the same vehicle model.
Cross-Product Cannibalization.formula: COUNT(*) where old lease early_termination_flag = TRUE, old and new model_name match, and lease actual_end date precedes loan origination date.
Cross-Product Cannibalization.notes: Match by customer_sk and vehicle model, not by VIN, unless VIN is explicitly requested.

Dealer Commission Paid.description: Net commission amount paid or planned for a dealer or broker.
Dealer Commission Paid.date_basis: Use payment value date for paid commission; use cashflow due date for planned commission.
Dealer Commission Paid.notes: Filter dim_cashflow_type.is_dealer_cashflow_flag = TRUE and is_fee_component_flag = TRUE.

Dealer Legal Name.description: Registered legal entity name of a dealer or broker.
Dealer Legal Name.notes: Use dim_dealer.legal_entity_name when the question asks for legal names or registered legal entities; use dim_dealer.dealer_name only for commercial dealer names. Legal entity names can repeat across technical dealer records, so use DISTINCT when the requested output is legal names rather than dealer records.

Dealer Risk Rating.description: Internal dealer risk rating stored on the dealer dimension.
Dealer Risk Rating.notes: Available generated values are LOW, MODERATE, ELEVATED, and HIGH. Risk severity increases in that order, so HIGH is the strongest or riskiest risk rating. Current dealer means dim_dealer.is_current_record = TRUE; do not add dealer_status_code = 'Active' unless the question explicitly says active dealer. Use categorical profiling when exact available values are uncertain.

Customer Risk Class.description: Internal underwriting risk class stored on the customer dimension.
Customer Risk Class.notes: Generated values are A, B, C, D, and E. Risk severity increases alphabetically, so A is the best risk class and E is the riskiest. Use categorical profiling when exact available values are uncertain.

Dormant Dealer.description: An active dealer that has not originated any leasing or loan contract in the requested period.
Dormant Dealer.formula: Active current dealer with no matching rows in fact_leasing_contracts or fact_loan_contracts for the period.
Dormant Dealer.date_basis: Use contract_start_date_sk for leasing and origination_date_sk for loans.
Dormant Dealer.notes: Current active dealer means dim_dealer.is_current_record = TRUE and dealer_status_code = 'Active'.

Durchschnittliche Vertragslaufzeit.formula: AVG(contract_term_months) for leasing or AVG(loan_term_months) for loans.
Durchschnittliche Vertragslaufzeit.date_basis: Use booking/start/origination date according to the requested portfolio.
Durchschnittliche Vertragslaufzeit.notes: The leasing and loan fact tables use different term column names.

Effective Interest Rate.formula: Use fact_leasing_contracts.interest_rate_effective for leasing and fact_loan_contracts.annual_percentage_rate_effective for loans.
Effective Interest Rate.date_basis: Use the contract's origination, booking, or start date requested by the question.
Effective Interest Rate.notes: Do not use nominal interest when the question asks for APR or effective customer rate. Loan book means the loan fact table population; add an active-status filter only when the question explicitly asks for active, current, performing, or active portfolio contracts.

Early Termination Rate.description: Share of contracts ended before scheduled maturity.
Early Termination Rate.formula: COUNT(CASE WHEN early_termination_flag THEN 1 END) / COUNT(*)
Early Termination Rate.date_basis: Use actual_end_date_sk for realized terminations unless the question asks by origination cohort.
Early Termination Rate.notes: Leasing and loan facts both expose early_termination_flag.

Gross Financed Amount.description: Financed amount including taxes or fees.
Gross Financed Amount.date_basis: Use booking_date_sk, contract_start_date_sk, or origination_date_sk as requested.
Gross Financed Amount.notes: Use net financed amount only when the question explicitly asks for net.

High-LTV Exposure.description: Loan contracts whose effective loan-to-value ratio exceeds the benchmark's high-LTV threshold.
High-LTV Exposure.date_basis: Use loan origination date for period analysis.
High-LTV Exposure.notes: Ratios are stored as decimals; the synthetic benchmark uses 0.8 as the high-LTV cutoff because no generated loans exceed 1.0.

Late Lease Installment.description: A planned recurring lease cashflow paid after its due date, optionally linked to late-fee payment transactions.
Late Lease Installment.formula: Compare fact_contract_cashflows.due_date_sk with the matching payment value_date_sk for non-reversed payments linked by related_contract_cashflow_sk.
Late Lease Installment.date_basis: Use due_date_sk for the scheduled installment period and fact_payment_transactions.value_date_sk for the actual receipt date.
Late Lease Installment.notes: Lease installments are recurring planned cashflows with related_leasing_contract_sk populated. Late-fee cash is represented by payment transactions classified as late fees, commonly transaction_type_code = 'LATE_FEE_COLLECTION', and should exclude reversal rows.

Insurance Bundle Penetration.description: Share or count of active products/contracts with an insurance bundle.
Insurance Bundle Penetration.date_basis: Use product status or contract period depending on the question.

Maturity Cohort.description: Contracts whose scheduled maturity falls in the requested period.
Maturity Cohort.formula: Join maturity_date_sk to dim_date and filter the requested period.
Maturity Cohort.notes: Do not use actual_end_date_sk unless the question asks for actual endings.

Missed Installment.description: A planned recurring cashflow with no matching non-reversed payment transaction.
Missed Installment.formula: Recurring planned cashflow where NOT EXISTS non-reversed fact_payment_transactions linked by related_contract_cashflow_sk.
Missed Installment.date_basis: Use fact_contract_cashflows.due_date_sk for the missed month.
Missed Installment.notes: Recurring installments are identified through dim_cashflow_type.is_recurring_flag = TRUE. Consecutive missed calendar months are evaluated per customer unless the question explicitly says per contract.

Net Current Balance.description: Net current balance value stored on account snapshot facts.
Net Current Balance.date_basis: Use snapshot_date_sk for explicit time-window, latest-snapshot, or as-of questions only.
Net Current Balance.notes: The word current is part of the measure name current_balance_net and does not by itself mean latest snapshot. For questions such as across all banking account snapshots or for customers with a channel, aggregate all matching snapshot rows unless the question explicitly asks for latest, current snapshot, as of a date, or one row per account. Do not substitute available_balance_effective unless the question asks for available funds.

Neugeschaeftsvolumen.description: Sum of financing volume for newly originated or booked contracts.
Neugeschaeftsvolumen.date_basis: Use contract_start_date_sk for leasing and origination_date_sk for loans unless the question says booking date.
Neugeschaeftsvolumen.notes: Use gross financed amount for the KPI unless the question explicitly asks for net new business.

Outstanding Principal.description: Planned principal not yet settled by successful non-reversed payments.
Outstanding Principal.date_basis: Use due_date_sk for planned principal due periods.
exclusions: Reversed payment transactions are not successful settlement.
Outstanding Principal.notes: Aggregate only payments with is_reversal_flag = FALSE by related_contract_cashflow_sk before subtracting from planned principal. When the question asks for principal scheduled in a period after successful payments are deducted, filter the planned cashflows by due_date_sk and subtract all successful payments linked to those cashflows; do not additionally constrain the payment value_date_sk unless the question asks when cash was received. Do not subtract reversal rows unless the question explicitly asks to account for signed reversal rows.

Overdraft Exposure.description: Accounts whose effective available balance is below zero.
Overdraft Exposure.notes: For authorized overdraft limits use authorized_overdraft_limit_net; for actual overdrawn state use available_balance_effective.

aliases: crossed from positive available balance into overdraft
Overdraft Run.description: Consecutive account snapshot days where available balance is below zero after an account crosses from positive available balance into overdraft.
Overdraft Run.formula: Find accounts with LAG(available_balance_effective) > 0 and available_balance_effective < 0, then segment all negative available_balance_effective snapshots by account and count each consecutive overdrawn segment.
Overdraft Run.date_basis: Order fact_banking_accounts snapshots by snapshot_date_sk joined to dim_date.full_date.
Overdraft Run.notes: Use available_balance_effective < 0 for overdraft state. To calculate the longest run, group consecutive negative snapshot rows into segments; non-negative rows break a segment. The crossing condition selects eligible accounts, but the longest run is the maximum negative segment length for each eligible account.

Payment Reversal Rate.description: Share of original non-reversed inflow amount that was later reversed.
Payment Reversal Rate.date_basis: Use original payment value_date_sk unless the question asks for reversal booking date.
Payment Reversal Rate.notes: Use ABS(reversal.amount_gross) when reporting the share of original inflow value that was reversed.

Plan-Ist-Zinsabweichung.description: Difference between planned gross interest and actually settled non-reversed gross interest.
Plan-Ist-Zinsabweichung.date_basis: Use due_date_sk for planned interest and value_date_sk for actual interest.
Plan-Ist-Zinsabweichung.notes: Compare gross interest components, not total payment amounts. For retail loan questions, join fact_loan_contracts to dim_customer and filter dim_customer.customer_type_code = 'Retail'.

Point-in-Time Attribute.description: A dimension attribute as it was valid for a fact row at the business event time.
Point-in-Time Attribute.formula: Use the historized surrogate key already stored on the fact row.
Point-in-Time Attribute.date_basis: Use the event date only for filtering the cohort, not for rejoining current dimension records.
Point-in-Time Attribute.notes: Do not filter is_current_record when the question asks for attributes at origination, booking, or snapshot time. Leasing and loan fact tables already store the historized dealer_sk and customer_sk valid at booking/origination; use those surrogate keys directly for point-in-time dealer or customer attributes such as dealer risk rating valid at origination.

Residual Value Gap.description: Difference between nominal and effective residual value for leasing contracts.
Residual Value Gap.date_basis: Use maturity or contract start date according to the question.
Residual Value Gap.notes: Positive values mean nominal residual value exceeds effective residual value.

Returned Refinanced Vehicle.description: Vehicles from successfully ended leasing contracts that were returned and are currently present in an active financing contract.
Returned Refinanced Vehicle.formula: Count returned fact_leasing_contracts rows with closed non-defaulted status and actual_end_date_sk in the requested period where the same vehicle_sk appears in any active leasing or loan contract.
Returned Refinanced Vehicle.date_basis: Use fact_leasing_contracts.actual_end_date_sk for the ended lease period; use current contract_status_sk joined to dim_contract_status for active follow-up contracts.
Returned Refinanced Vehicle.notes: Do not require the active follow-up contract to have the same customer. Do not require termination_reason_code unless the question explicitly asks for a specific termination reason. A successfully ended lease is closed and not defaulted, with vehicle_return_date_sk populated.

aliases: newly financed vehicle models after early lease end
newly financed vehicle models after customers previously ended a lease early
new loan after early lease termination
Newly Financed Vehicle After Early Lease.description: Vehicle models financed by new loan contracts after the same customer previously ended a lease early.
Newly Financed Vehicle After Early Lease.formula: Match fact_loan_contracts to earlier fact_leasing_contracts for the same customer where old lease early_termination_flag = TRUE and old actual_end_date precedes loan origination date.
Newly Financed Vehicle After Early Lease.notes: Newly financed means a new loan contract in this benchmark context, not a later lease. Count the vehicle model on the new loan contract.

Stornoquote.description: Share of cancelled contracts among all considered contracts.
Stornoquote.formula: COUNT(CASE WHEN dim_contract_status.is_cancelled_flag THEN 1 END) / COUNT(contract_id)
Stornoquote.date_basis: Use the period requested by the question.
exclusions: Contracts cancelled before becoming effective, for example same-day cancellation before activation.
Stornoquote.notes: Join fact_leasing_contracts or fact_loan_contracts to dim_contract_status via contract_status_sk.

Successful Payment.description: A booked payment transaction that has not itself been flagged as a reversal.
Successful Payment.date_basis: Use value_date_sk for cash receipt timing.
Successful Payment.notes: Successful receipt questions filter to is_reversal_flag = FALSE and do not subtract reversal rows. Use reversal rows only when the question explicitly asks for reversal rates, signed reversal rows, or accounting after reversals.

Vintage.description: A cohort grouped by the period in which a contract was originated or booked.
Vintage.formula: Group by the requested origination, start, or booking date dimension.
Vintage.date_basis: Use origination_date_sk for loans, contract_start_date_sk for leasing, and booking_date_sk only when explicitly requested.
Vintage.notes: Do not group by maturity date for vintage analysis.
